Class TableRowsSubwindow

java.lang.Object
ui.Subwindow
ui.TableRowsSubwindow
All Implemented Interfaces:
ScrollableWindow
public class TableRowsSubwindow extends Subwindow implements ScrollableWindow
A subwindow that displays and allows editing of rows in a table. Supports cell editing, boolean toggling, and row deletion.

  • Nested Class Summary

  • Constructor Details

    • TableRowsSubwindow

      public TableRowsSubwindow(int x, int y, int width, int height, String title, Table table)
      Constructs a new TableRowsSubwindow.
      Parameters:
      x - the x-coordinate of the window
      y - the y-coordinate of the window
      width - the width of the window
      height - the height of the window
      title - the window title
      table - the table to display rows for
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if table is null

  • Method Details

    • draw

      public void draw(Graphics g, boolean isActive)
      Draws the contents of the subwindow including headers, rows, and the UI elements.
      Specified by:
      draw in class Subwindow
      Parameters:
      g - the Graphics context to draw on
      isActive - whether this window is the active one

    • handleKeyEvent

      public void handleKeyEvent(int id, int keyCode, char keyChar)
      Handles key events like deletion of a row.
      Specified by:
      handleKeyEvent in class Subwindow
      Parameters:
      id - the key event ID
      keyCode - the key code
      keyChar - the key character

    • handleMouseEvent

      public void handleMouseEvent(int id, int mouseX, int mouseY, int clickCount)
      Handles mouse interactions for row selection, editing, and row creation.
      Specified by:
      handleMouseEvent in class Subwindow
      Parameters:
      id - the mouse event ID
      mouseX - the x-position of the mouse
      mouseY - the y-position of the mouse
      clickCount - the number of clicks
